title: mangifera monandra
intro: Mangifera monandra, also known as the Philippine mango or wild mango, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to the Philippines and Southeast Asia. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. The herbal preparations of Mangifera monandra include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be consumed as teas, capsules, or applied topically to the skin for its therapeutic effects.
